sys_hub_flow.description: Subflow that validates the Resolved -> Closed transition on x_casemgmt_case. Per AAP Section 0.5.5, requires the caller to have the x_casemgmt_case_manager role; calls x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToClosed(case_record, gs.getUserID()) which delegates to gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager'). On failure, throws the validator's error message "Only case managers can close cases." which surfaces on the form via Flow Designer Throw Error (gs.addErrorMessage + setAbortAction). On success, sets closed_date = gs.nowDateTime() on the case via an Update Record step (the AAP Section 0.5.5 mandated side effect of the Resolved -> Closed transition; defense-in-depth with ../../business_rules/x_casemgmt_set_closed_date.xml). Called by parent flows ../general_inquiry_state_machine.xml and ../complaint_state_machine.xml whenever a case status changes to 'Closed'. Inputs: case_record (Reference to x_casemgmt_case, mandatory, $trigger.current). No outputs at subflow level. ServiceNow-native replacement for ArkCase ChangeCaseFileStateService.changeCaseState's "if (CLOSED.equals(newState)) retval.setClosed(now)" branch (lines 97-100), strengthened with an explicit case_manager role gate that ArkCase did not have. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id reference literals.
sys_hub_flow.name: x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition
